transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Yeah, know.
Is there anybody else joining us today?
I don't think so.
Not on my end.
Okay, perfect.
I had a customer call today with, I think, seven or eight people.
So it's always nice to be one-on-one.
Honestly, it was a great, fantastic product, Deep Dive.
was really, yeah, really clarified just any questions that I had.
So the next steps after that are going to be to update the artifacts that we do have.

So the ones that you reviewed, if the messaging is going to come in this week that you're working on or early next week, well, we're going to update it right away and then update it, like iterate as new information comes in.
We also have another artifact for your review, which are the writing guidelines.
Okay.
I'll share those in Slack later.
The writing guidelines are...
going to be changed a lot.
Once we start creating content, we see what the output is.
So we kind of refine the writing out guidelines based on that and based on your feedback.

And then also we have a proofreading checklist, which is not something that you review, but any feedback that you provide around just small details like use contractions, don't use the M-dash, don't use semicolons, like these things, those go in the proofreading.
And then from the product deep dive transcript, we will also create a product feature matrix.
And that's going to be used to inform like the product messaging in the articles, because we don't want to rely on information from the internet to write articles about you.
And also like the company context is good foundation.
But what happens sometimes is that AI will sometimes make conclusions about your platform.
Based on what other platforms generally can do.
And then it's, you know, we've had situations before.
This was like maybe five or six months ago where a customer will say, well, there are some companies in our space that do this, but we don't.
And then we actually use the product messaging artifact as like the source of truth and fact check all the information.
Got it.
Got it.
Okay.
Yeah, that's helpful.
Awesome.
So, yeah, I mean, the big update this week is obviously like the first version of your content strategy.
We've done a lot of work there, and I also reviewed it yesterday with Marcel.
He shared some thoughts with me that I will also share with you today.

So the way we develop content strategies is, well, actually, before we dive into the content strategy, I wanted to kind of walk back a little bit and just repeat some of the things that we talked about last week regarding like what we are here to do.
And the main takeaway from our.

meeting last week was that, like, the primary thing that we're trying to fix here is helping your sales team have access to high-quality content that they can use for prospect education and also to build brand visibility in both traditional SEO and GEO.
Does that sound like a good summary?
Yes, definitely.
Okay, perfect.

And I think when we were, you know, when we, when we were talking to Nader, we got a lot of really good input on how he talks about, you know, your, your industry, what's the technical positioning, what's the sales narrative.
And we also got a lot of good input from the product Deep Dive today to help with your positioning in the market.
Obviously, with your space, it's incredibly competitive.
We need to differentiate you using, obviously, the feature that we covered today.
And you do have, like, an enterprise motion.
So, a lot of times, you know, the...
Companies that we work with, they really do look at very specific metrics, but this is still being built out over at Elementum.
So we will, like, you know, as your marketing org evolves, we will evolve with you.
So our primary goal really right now is to build authority, visibility, and, like, credibility with your prospects.
We always, mean, you know, even if you don't have a lot of content, we always do look at the website just to see what we're working with.
And the one thing that stood out for me is that your domain authority, as measured by SEMR, is pretty solid.
So it's, like, 29.
So it's a good baseline to work with.
So we're not, you know, ranking isn't going to be difficult compared to, like, when your domain authority is, like, brand new.
It was, like, five.
But obviously, the content that you have published so far, it's not getting a lot of traction.
And actually, we are.

Working on a calibration article for you right now, just so you can provide feedback on voice and tone, and we're refreshing one of your earlier articles that you published last year about business process automation, but obviously all of the traffic to your website is branded, and so this is not, you know, not a lot for us to refresh or, you know, do in terms of content optimization.

Then for every strategy that we build, we look at your competitors, and this is the process that Marcel built, and this is how this company got started, which is we are analyzing your competitors, not just direct competitors, but also maybe organic competitors, people who are competing for the same audience.
And we try to analyze their content library, extract topic clusters that are relevant for you, and then pick the best opportunities in each topic cluster for you to create content for.

One of the things that when we were discussing yesterday, well, actually, before I even started, when I started doing the research, you are in an interesting situation where you have like four different categories of competitors from the board deck that you shared.
So some of the websites that I was looking at was not just the website, the companies that you shared, but also really like authoritative websites that talk about AI agents.
For example, IBM has a huge, like a pretty big library and they just talk about AI agents.
And if you look Google AI agents, it's going to be just like, it's mostly IBM that's ranking for those very foundational keywords.
So I was always looking at IBM, also Zapier, they're like more foundational and they don't speak to a very technical audience, but it's also helpful to understand like what they are also talking.
And then the other competitors that we're looking, obviously, Glean, Automation Anywhere, also looked at UiPath, and their content is still heavily, like, oriented towards robotic process automation.
However, you know, as you're probably aware, they recently acquired WorkFusion, and now they're, like, laying it on thick with AI agent orchestration.
Even their homepage is just, like, putting orchestration front and center.
So we also factor them into the analysis.
Palantir, just a quick note on Palantir.
We didn't factor them into the analysis because they don't have SEO blog content on their website.

So their content is very product-driven or just pure thought leadership that comes from, you know, the executives on their team, where they talk about the company or their efforts, but they weren't a part of the analysis.
So overall, mean, we analyzed.
We like thousands, think over three, 4,000 URLs, and we were able to extract nine different topic clusters.
But this is a lot, obviously.
mean, nine is, it's a testament to like your platform because you have like horizontal use case and also vertical use case.
So if we were to start targeting all of those verticals, I mean, we could be, you could have a content library like IBM, like just absolutely, you know, thousands of pages.
And when I was talking to Marcel yesterday, we were discussing the strategy.
One of the things that he highlighted was that you are in a space that's very competitive.
And you're talking about a technical concept that people don't understand.
And we need to show that you're an authority.
And in order to do that, we do need to cover the foundations first and almost build like a study guide for your audience.
So what, how, the way we would do that is by focusing.
And just on agentic AI foundations first and AI workflow orchestration.
So those would be like the primary points of focus for, I would say, like probably like eight weeks to build a nice foundation there.
And that way we can also support the sales team with material to like share with prospects.
We could talk about Elementum and how you help orchestrate AI agents in different workflows.
But we wouldn't be going into those different vertical topics just yet because we want to make sure that we have like a good foundation here.
And then once we get into those different verticals, we can also link to these articles.
So it supports like a nice internal linking structure as well.
Does that make sense to you?
Yeah, it makes a ton of sense.
The one thing that I do feel like we want to get to early on in the strategy, though, is starting to land this.
And I think I mentioned it just in

In a comment yesterday, what we think is going to be kind of a contrarian view, which is that agents are being used too much and that you're actually doing harm by using agents for things that they're not appropriate for.
Both because of the probabilistic nature and because it's too expensive in terms of dollars and in terms of resource usage, right?
All the way down to there's not enough power in the world for people to be abusing agents.
Exactly.
Yeah, absolutely.
Yeah, I think we can definitely incorporate that.
So every cluster comes with like a brief description of what the focus is going to be for Elementum.
So I think we can expand this with those points and say like AI agents aren't for everything.
Yeah, exactly.
For, I mean, I provided just a couple of like example keywords.
From competitors just to show what like the potential search volume is, but next week we're going to prepare like a whole content calendar with like individual topics.
So we're going to get into like the more specifics next week.
For AI workflow orchestration specifically, this is cluster where IBM and Anthropic have a lot of like really authoritative content around.
But there are, you know, this is kind of an emerging category in the sense that there's not a lot of like search volume when you're talking about like AI agent orchestration.
The search volume on a global level is quite low.
It's like 490 per month compared to like some other AI related topics.
So it is an emerging topic.
So publishing foundational content here would be great.
And this is where we could also talk about like agent sprawl, how, you know, companies have all of these like agents that are basically unusable or they.
have these failed AI agent implementations because they don't have a way to orchestrate those agents.
And this is where we would also get into like the deterministic versus probabilistic models and how that impacts outcomes when using AI workflows.
So these are the two that we would prioritize at first, but we also have the other seven outlined as well.
So you can kind of see what we were able to find and what would the progression be from these two clusters moving forward.
So the third one would just focus on AI governance.

This one speaks to executives who are concerned about all of the AI agents that are running wild in their ecosystems and what they should look for, how they can observe those agents and position Elementum as a way to kind of gain control and have like a unified platform where you can like employees can execute.
Things like the runtime of each step is less relevant from a standpoint, but that is functionality.
Like in that specific example, if you tried to turn over a decision like, should I grant this appeal or not, and you just invoke an agent, that's, it's illegal, right?
I mean, you can't have a black box process for something like that.
And so the observability view is a really big deal.
Okay, okay, that's fantastic.
Definitely going to work that into the focus here.
And I think here we could also talk about zero persistence on the platform.
The other clusters specifically, I think there are three.
So there's one for IT service management that go into the different use cases.
This would just, would just focus on the role of AI in supporting IT service management.
for service So there's one IT

How to implement IT service management, obviously position Elementum as a way to do that without having to get a bespoke platform for that purpose, and this is a way to support one of your important use cases, and this is where ServiceNow and ICERA dominate in terms of rankings, so they do have pretty established content libraries that we can use to displace their visibility.

And then also, I mean, here, like, another big point that we could talk about is how, like, the different cost savings that are associated with using Elementum and how you have, like, greater cost visibility into just how expensive, like, IT service management is.
Yeah, got it.
The fifth one would just focus on procurement and source-to-pay automation.
This supports another important use case category, and this is where, I mean, from our...
Initial conversations, I know that you replaced Coupa at one of your clients.
We might not talk about it publicly, but we can kind of, you know, demonstrate the use case, talk about how Elementum serves these types of use cases.
And this is where we also kind of lay the groundwork for GEO visibility for these types of use cases.
We complement that with very specific comparison content with these types of platforms.

But the more content you have on your website that talks about these use cases, the easier it is for LLMs and also Google's algorithm to associate you with this type of, providing this type of platform.
So this is just another way we would support that use case.
Yeah, got it.
And one important related topic here, and I think we maybe talked about this a little bit with Nader, is that at this point, we don't really want to lean in.
And hard on, hey, Elementum's here to take out ServiceNow and to take out Coupa.
The way that we typically start is we layer on top of them.

And so it's a better way, it's a simpler way to interact with Coupa, as an example, where we're actually the front door, we take in a request, and what we're doing is we're actually passing that request to Coupa and potentially adding workflow steps in, maybe adding some approvals, and then actually writing the record to Coupa.

What we tend to find is that when companies do that, they realize, well, actually, as I move more and more workloads over to Elementum, it just makes sense to not maintain Coupa as the system of record.
And so we get there eventually, but we don't really lead with that message.
We don't, you know, at this point in the go-to-market, it probably doesn't feel credible for us to, for us to come
I'm charging in and say, we're going to take out all your SaaS applications.
Yeah, yeah, absolutely.
Now, that's a fair point.
think once we do get to covering this type of content, it's going to be further down the line.
So the positioning might change.
Yeah, exactly.
The sixth one would just focus on the role of AI in the pharmaceutical and life science industry.
This is a vertical topic cluster for one of, I think, I believe it's your most important vertical.
There's a lot in terms of content volume here.
Appian has a lot of content here around these types of topics, just more broadly around AI use cases and pharmaceutical supply chain.
So there's a lot of different types of topics that we could cover here and then tie that back to Elementum and how Elementum can make things easier.
Thank you.

And just the example that Vlad had in the demo, I actually used to do competitive intelligence for a pharmaceutical company, and I know how busy salespeople are, because I had to call them all day and try to get them on the phone, so they would talk to me, incredibly busy.
So this is a really kind of a great example that we could also use for this type of content.
We could also talk about, you know, the patent cliff for different drugs.
So there's lots of topics that we could cover here.
The seventh one is, it's closely related, it is related to AI workflow orchestration, and I think it's one that makes maybe the most sense to transition into from like the main two.

You already do have an article around business process automation, but this would cover topics that aren't AI specific, where we would capture, you know, searchers who are generally exploring automation as a way to solve.
the problems that they're facing at work.
They might not know that they could use AI agent orchestration or AI agents to solve any of those problems.
So this is where we would kind of capture those people.
This is where Zapier has a lot of presence.
I believe IBM as well.
For IBM, were just like mostly focusing on agents as a main topic.

This is somewhere where like at some point in time in the future, we can revisit this, but kind of weaving in the Sasspocalypse narrative that we were talking about last week around how, you know, Sass is being replaced.
But again, this is something that we can discuss once we do start creating this type of content for this cluster.
Yeah.
Okay.
And another thing about business process automation, obviously, because it's not a new concept by any means, there's, this is the broadest
And the most competitive cluster in the entire set.
So I believe almost all of the companies that we analyzed had some type of content on this topic.
And the search volume and the number of topics that we could cover here is quite, quite broad.
The eighth one would focus on another use case that we were discussing last week, which is using Elementum as a CRM.
Obviously, I think here it would also be relevant, the point that you just made around how you don't replace a CRM, you can work with a CRM.
All right.

Yeah, so I think this is another cluster where we could talk about different processes and CRMs like sales processes, how to onboard customers, how to manage pipeline, and tie that back to Elementum workflows and how they can make those processes easier and faster.
Um, and I think this is, I think AI...
And as AI agents and just AI workflow in general, they have like entered sales platforms and have been there for a while.
And I think this use case in particular, they're feeling the pain of like just maybe not that useful AI tools.
So I think this is where we could really kind of highlight how AI agents really don't work for everything.
You want to have specific like business rules in place to execute certain workflows.
So I think this is, we just pulled out a couple of examples here.
Obviously, Salesforce just leads the pack here just because they're in that vertical.
But yeah, there's a lot, a lot here that we could also pull from.
And then the last one would just focus on competitive comparisons.
Again, this is, you know, this is something that we have to treat, you know, mindfully.
We don't want to compare you to ServiceNow or...
Coupa, or these other platforms just yet because you're not at that stage.

But when we're talking about AI workflow automation tools or workflow orchestration tools around which there is search volume and companies are already targeting those keywords, I think you should definitely show up in that set because it's certainly a category that you want to own and there's no reason why we don't want to rent for those keywords just now.
And then later on, we can explore like the comparisons and alternatives to different tools that you're replacing.
Yeah, agreed.
All right.
So this is a lot.
Any top like high level feedback that you have right now, obviously, I'll share this link with you and then you can also leave comments as you go through it.
Yeah, I agree with you that there's just a huge amount of surface area here.
Topics one and two.
two.
Definitely right to start with.
And especially when I put the lens on of what's going to be most useful for the sales team in the short term, a lot of that content is going to be perfect.
It's going to be really, really shareable.
I mean, you take an example of the topic of deterministic versus probabilistic workflows, that kind of asset will be super helpful to the team.
Yeah.
So I think the surface area is huge and the first two are clearly the highest priority.
Yeah.
And for when I was talking to Marcel yesterday, one of the things he brought up is that for this type of content, we have a lot of really authoritative websites that we could link to.
And we've been doing some analysis internally of content that tends to perform really well in LLMs and some of the.

Early results are indicating that the websites that do get cited the most are those with, you know, a lot of, like, citations, meaning, like, they are linking to other websites or linking to, like, research or just, like, different reports.
And for this type of educational content, there's just so much that we could pull from.
Yeah, agreed.
Yeah.
All right, perfect.
So that is kind of the starting point.
Like I said, right now, our focus is going to be on researching topics for all of those clusters.
Like, we're not going to wait for, like, eight weeks to, like, get started.
We want to find, start finding opportunities now.
But we're going to focus more on the primary two clusters.
And then at the same time, we're also working on a calibration article for you, which is a refresh of the business process automation piece that we'll share with you, I believe, early next week.
We want to make sure that we, like, we're testing the pipeline for the first time.
And we still need the product feature matrix, so we want to set everything up well before we send you any content.
And then you can provide feedback on voice and tone and just positioning, and we'll work all of that feedback in.
Let me just go back to my agenda.

I know we have like two minutes left, but on the performance tracking side, we've also set up your Looker Dashboard, which is just going to provide insight into organic search traffic and also generally the data that's collected in Google Analytics.
We use it to measure just the performance of the content that we publish on your blog.

We also have an LLM referral page set up in there, so we can see how many clicks are coming to your site from different LLMs, which is also helpful to understand how our content is performing in different LLMs.
We can do a walkthrough of that next week, or I can record a loom.
We'll have access to that, and we're also going to set up your workspace.
Check that, which is our geo visibility platform.
So that's also going to, that's also coming your way.
It's in development.
So we're going to use that to just measure your overall AI visibility and measure how many times our content is being cited in LLMs as well.
Perfect.
Okay.
Any questions?
I don't think so.
I don't think so.
We covered a ton of ground.
I know.
Yeah.

I, it's, it's a lot, but once we ramp up with the publishing and the sharing of the content, it starts to, like right now, it's all a little bit abstract, but once we really start creating the content and finding the topics, it's going to, we're going to dig into your feedback as well.
Perfect.
All right.
Well, thank you so much for your time, Andrew.
I'll send a follow-up in Slack in a little bit.
And I also shared the new writing guidelines.
Okay.
Awesome.
Thanks a lot, Aida.
Thank you.
Bye.
We'll see you.
Bye-bye.-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review the proposed content strategy and align on initial priorities.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=146.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Initial Focus:** Prioritize foundational content on "Agentic AI Foundations" and "AI Workflow Orchestration" for \~8 weeks to build authority and provide immediate sales enablement.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=507.0)
  - [**Core Narrative:** Frame Elementum as an orchestrator that layers *on top* of existing SaaS (e.g., ServiceNow, Coupa), not a direct replacement, to build credibility.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1010.0)
  - [**Contrarian View:** Integrate the narrative that agents are overused, expensive, and inappropriate for deterministic tasks, positioning Elementum as the solution for controlled, observable workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=588.0)
  - [**Content Pipeline:** A "Business Process Automation" article refresh will serve as a calibration piece to test the content pipeline and align on voice/tone before full production begins.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1545.0)

## Topics

### Content Strategy Overview

  - [**Goal:** Provide high-quality content for sales enablement and build brand visibility via SEO and GEO (Generative Engine Optimization).](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=177.0)
  - [**Baseline:** A Domain Authority (DA) of 29 provides a strong foundation for ranking, but current traffic is almost entirely branded.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=263.0)
  - [**Competitor Analysis:** Analyzed 3,000+ URLs from competitors (IBM, Zapier, UiPath, Glean) to identify content gaps.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=329.0)
      - [**Note:** Palantir was excluded due to its lack of SEO blog content.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=456.0)

### Proposed Content Clusters

  - [**1. Agentic AI Foundations (Priority 1)**](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=527.0)
      - [**Focus:** Core concepts of agentic AI.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=527.0)
      - [**Narrative:** Integrate the contrarian view on agent misuse (probabilistic nature, high cost).](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=588.0)
      - [**Sales Value:** Provide shareable assets (e.g., deterministic vs. probabilistic workflows).](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1442.0)
  - [**2. AI Workflow Orchestration (Priority 2)**](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=674.0)
      - [**Focus:** The emerging category of orchestrating AI agents.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=674.0)
      - [**Opportunity:** Low global search volume (\~490/mo) → chance to own the category.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=674.0)
      - [**Narrative:** Address "agent sprawl" and failed implementations from lack of orchestration.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=710.0)
  - [**3. AI Governance**](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=754.0)
      - [**Focus:** Executive concerns about agent control and observability.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=754.0)
      - [**Narrative:** Position Elementum as the unified platform for control, highlighting observability and zero data persistence.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=754.0)
  - [**4. IT Service Management (ITSM)**](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=882.0)
      - [**Focus:** AI's role in ITSM, targeting ServiceNow and ICERA.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=882.0)
      - [**Narrative:** Position Elementum as a cost-effective layer on top of existing ITSM platforms.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=886.0)
  - [**5. Procurement & Source-to-Pay Automation**](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=950.0)
      - [**Focus:** AI in procurement, targeting Coupa.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=950.0)
      - [**Narrative:** Position Elementum as a layer that simplifies interaction with platforms like Coupa, adding workflow and approvals.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1010.0)
  - [**6. Pharma & Life Sciences**](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1100.0)
      - [**Focus:** AI use cases in Elementum's most important vertical.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1100.0)
      - [**Narrative:** Use real-world examples (e.g., busy salespeople, patent cliff) to demonstrate value.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
  - [**7. Business Process Automation (BPA)**](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1171.0)
      - [**Focus:** Capture searchers exploring automation generally.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1171.0)
      - [**Note:** This is the broadest and most competitive cluster.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1251.0)
  - [**8. CRM & Sales Automation**](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1276.0)
      - [**Focus:** AI in sales processes, targeting Salesforce.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1276.0)
      - [**Narrative:** Highlight the pain of ineffective AI tools in sales and showcase Elementum's rule-based workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1316.0)
  - [**9. Competitive Comparisons**](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1364.0)
      - [**Focus:** Comparison content.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1364.0)
      - [**Strategy:** Start with direct AI workflow orchestration competitors to own the category, then expand to larger platforms (ServiceNow, Coupa) later.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1364.0)

### Content Development Process

  - [**Product Deep Dive:** A recent call with Elementum clarified product details.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=13.0)
  - [**Artifacts:**](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=28.0)
      - [**Product Feature Matrix:** A source-of-truth document to ensure factual accuracy and prevent AI hallucinations.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=89.0)
      - [**Writing Guidelines:** Shared in Slack for initial review.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=48.0)
      - [**Proofreading Checklist:** Internal document for style consistency (e.g., contractions, em-dashes).](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=72.0)
  - [**Calibration Article:** A refresh of the "Business Process Automation" article will be shared early next week to test the content pipeline and align on voice/tone.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1545.0)

### Performance Tracking

  - [**Looker Dashboard:** Set up to track organic search traffic and Google Analytics data.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1577.0)
      - [Includes an LLM referral page to measure clicks from generative engines.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1598.0)
  - [**Check That:** A GEO visibility platform in development to measure LLM citations and overall AI visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1613.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Aida:**](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1667.0)
      - [Share the content strategy document and writing guidelines in Slack.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=48.0)
      - [Share the "Business Process Automation" calibration article early next week.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1545.0)
      - [Record a Loom video walkthrough of the Looker Dashboard.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1577.0)
  - [**Andrew:**](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1417.0)
      - [Review the content strategy and writing guidelines, providing comments in the documents.](https://fathom.video/share/wDLUBPyddU-1sGDnNpQECiqcrqSvSYzv?tab=summary&timestamp=1417.0)
